turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey Sarah, I was looking at our CRM system the other day. You know how we've been complaining about the slow load times and all the glitches? I found this other platform, Zoho CRM. It's way cheaper, has better integrations, and the reviews say it's faster. Should we switch?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Oh yeah, Mike? I've heard of Zoho. Let me think... We've been on Salesforce for like four years now, right? Um, switching sounds good in theory, but have you thought about the hassle?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hassle? Like what? The migration tools look pretty straightforward. They even offer free support for the first month. And get this, it could save us like 30% on licensing fees.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, savings are nice, but migrating all our customer data? That's thousands of records. Remember last time we tried updating the database? It took weeks and half our team was pulling their hair out.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: True, that was a mess, but this isn't the same. Zoho has APIs that integrate directly with Salesforce exports. Plus, their demos show real-time syncing. I even watched a YouTube video where a company our size switched in under a week.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hmm, videos are one thing. But okay, let's say it works. What about training? Everyone's trained on Salesforce. The interface is muscle memory now. We'd have to do workshops, cheat sheets... and productivity dips for a month at least.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Productivity dip? Come on, the UI is more intuitive according to Gartner reports. New hires would pick it up faster anyway. And long-term, no more downtime complaints from sales.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Gartner, sure. But look, Mike, I get it – Zoho scores higher on those benchmarks. Faster, better mobile app, AI features we don't have. Evidence is there. But... switching costs, man. Not just money – time, training, risk of data loss. What if something breaks during migration and we lose leads?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Risky, yeah. But they have backups and rollback options. We could test on a small subset first.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Test subset? That's smart, but even then, customizing workflows to match Salesforce? Our custom fields, automations – we'd have to rebuild half of them. Dev team is already swamped with the app update. And contracts: we're locked in till end of year, early termination fees are brutal.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Oof, forgot about the contract. Still, post-year, why not? The evidence is overwhelming – Zoho's uptime is 99.9% vs our 98.5%, and support response is hours not days.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Exactly, evidence says switch. But those switching costs... let's ballpark it. Migration: 2 weeks dev time at $10k. Training: $5k plus lost sales hours, say $15k total dip. Fees: $20k. That's $50k easy, vs $10k annual savings. Payback in 5 years? And that's if nothing goes wrong. Tangent: remember the email switch two years ago? Promised paradise, ended up with spam hell for weeks.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Yeah, that sucked. Okay, maybe pilot it for one department? Marketing could test.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Pilot fragments data – sales needs unified view. No go. Look, I hate Salesforce too, but inertia wins here. Switching costs override the 'better' evidence until we hit a crisis. Let's stick and negotiate better support instead. Deal?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Sigh, fine. You're right, too much hassle for now. But bookmark Zoho for next budget cycle.

input.objective: Product manager A pushes to switch CRM from Salesforce to superior Zoho, but B cites high switching costs to stick with status quo
